content: Battle.net OAuth 2.0: Fully documented at community.developer.battle.net. Standard OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Without additional scopes: returns account ID and BattleTag only. WoW scope (wow.profile) and SC2 scope (sc2.profile) exist. NO WC3-specific scope exists in Battle.net API - Warcraft 3 is not a supported game in Blizzard's profile APIs. W3champions uses Battle.net OAuth to get BattleTag, then looks up the player in their OWN database using that BattleTag as identifier. Auth.js (nextauth) has a BattleNet provider. Django allauth has battlenet provider. So the OAuth flow is: Battle.net login -> get BattleTag -> query w3champions API with BattleTag -> get w3champions stats. This is feasible and proven (w3champions itself does it). Rate limits: unclear from research but w3champions uses caching extensively in their architecture.
